title: トレセン学園百人一首クイーン決定戦

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
One day, I head to the classroom searching for
Grass Wonder.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Ahem, here we go. "I think upon you, as I—"

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text: Outta the way, Spe! I GOT THIS!

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: Aaaaah!

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
El, that isn't even close to the correct card. You
just earned yourself a penalty.

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text: Huh? I coulda sworn the poem went like that.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Oh, Trainer. The academy is holding a karuta
contest soon, so we're practicing matching poetry
verses to their continuations.

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: That's right! Grass is really, really good!

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: But, um... El is a little—

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
A little what?! It's not like you're any better at
this, Spe!

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
How are we supposed to remember all these
so-called poems anyways? They all just sound like
lazy run-on sentences to me!

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
You couldn't be more mistaken, El. Each one tells
a unique story. This one, for instance...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
I think upon you, as I take to the springtide
fields, harvesting new greens. The sleeve of my
robe collects its own share of falling snow.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
It paints a scene of someone gathering fresh
greens for the one they love as winter fades into
spring.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
There's something beautiful about how it depicts
not the peak of spring, but the subtle transition
between seasons.

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text:
Huh... I never would've guessed. If you ask me,
snow during springtime sounds like a pain...

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
What's beautiful about pickin' veggies? I mean I
could maaaybe see it if they were red-hot
peppers or something fun, but c'mon...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Hehe. That would get you excited, wouldn't it?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Still, what I want to say is: visualizing what the
poem depicts can make memorizing it easier.

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: Ah! You might be right! You're amazing, Grass!

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
All right, Grass! Do another round! I'll snag the
right card for sure this time!

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Hehe. Very well.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Oh, I know. This game is more fun with more
players. Would you care to join us, Trainer?
choice_data_list: I'll take you up on that.

I'll take over as game master so you can play,
Grass.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Great. I'll continue as the reader, then.

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: I hope you're good at this, Grass's Trainer!

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
Hehehe. Bet ya can't flip the cards faster than me
though!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
And so, I join them all playing the verse-matching
card game.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Hehe. This one is absolutely exquisite, so please
try to visualize the story as you listen.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
It portrays the traditional Japanese appreciation
for fleeting beauty, illustrated in the contrast
between—

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text:
Graaass! It's awesome that you know so much,
but...

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
This is gonna take forever if ya keep explainin'
each one before we start!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The game lasts longer than everyone had
intended, but Grass Wonder is beaming with joy
the entire time.

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text:
Oh, good idea! After all that practice, we might
be able to put up a fight against Grass!

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
Uh, S-Spe?! You probably don't wanna go saying
things like—

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Well, isn't that an idea? Thank you, Trainer.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
And of course, I'm sure nobody will mind if I give
this my all. ♪

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: Why does her smile look so scary?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
And with that, the poem-matching card game
recommences.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
There—it seems like I've won. El, you didn't even
manage to claim a single card.

text_block_list.name: El Condor Pasa
text_block_list.text:
NGGGHHH! One more round! We're doin' this
again!

text_block_list.name: Special Week
text_block_list.text: I knew Grass was gonna be good, but this good? I don't think anyone could beat her.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The way Grass Wonder dominated this practice
session, she should be unstoppable in the contest.
